betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Monastic bees aren't found in hives or bred. Instead, you will need to find a Beekeeper villager and trade with them for it. There are various ways to make more villagers, so do some research.

[note]Trader's Notes:

Beekeeper villagers spawn in houses that have a fenced back yard with apiaries. If the apiaries are missing, the villager didn't spawn. Or, well, a player already came along and looted the apiaries. They might have abducted the beekeeper too. The beekeeper should be wearing an apiarist outfit. Their initial trades probably don't have this bee. You will have to work up to this by trading what is available. If they require a princess for a trade, an ignoble one is fine. Honorable mention goes to the trade of some logs for an apiary. That's so much easier than having to make them yourself! If trades lock up, one trade of the "rightmost" trade should unlock preceding ones so you can keep trying to get this trade to pop up.

You can make a golden lasso at LV tier and run off with the villager. If you do, prepare a heavily-fortified spot. Even single-block-wide walls won't keep all zombies away. Consider just keeping the beekeeper in the lasso when you're not using them.

Beekeeper's Notes:

The base fertility for a Monastic bee is 1x. That's really annoying. When each cycle ends, you get a princess and a drone. That's it. You don't get surplus stock for carrying on breeding. If you can't just straight up alter the genome, then you can try to convert an arctic princess (or something else with a high fertility) with monastic drones in a way that gives you a higher fertility.

On the flip side, their long life is useful to try to get into production bees so you don't quickly clog up your bee product inventories with drones.

The "monastic line" of bees have other quirks. They'll take stuff like wheat and bookshelves as pollination sources. They can have some nasty aggression habits. They are long-lived, so you want something that cuts their life cycle for breeding--like an oblivion frame. You will start out here with a bee that likes average temperatures and humidity, but that'll drift to outright hellish and arid (but not The Nether!) for descendant species.
[/note]
